concepts.label: Assessment campaign
concepts.summary: A bounded review exercise for a defined organization, scope, time window, and set of controls.
concepts.why_it_exists: Assessments organize audit work into a repeatable exercise instead of leaving controls and evidence disconnected.
how_to_use: Create an assessment for a specific review period.
Limit it to one scope when the audit is not organization-wide.
Select the controls that will be checked during that exercise.

concepts.label: Assessment review
concepts.summary: The review record for one control inside one assessment.
concepts.why_it_exists: Reviews are where the actual audit work gets recorded: result, notes, conclusion, evidence, and any finding raised from the test.
how_to_use: Open the checklist for the assessment.
Record the result for each control you test.
Attach workpapers and create a finding when the control has a gap.

concepts.label: Assessment workpaper
concepts.summary: A file attached to a control review inside an assessment.
concepts.why_it_exists: Workpapers keep the supporting sample, screenshot, export, or note package together with the review that used it.
how_to_use: Upload the file from the control review panel.
Give it a clear label so the reviewer knows what it proves.
Use workpapers to support the conclusion or the linked finding.

guide.title: Run an assessment
guide.summary: Use assessments to group the controls you want to review in one audit cycle.
body: Start by choosing the organization, optional scope, and timeframe.
Then select the controls that belong to the review.
Use the campaign list to track whether the exercise is still in draft, active, or already closed.
Open the checklist to record control results, upload workpapers, and raise findings.

relationships.summary: An assessment campaign groups the controls that will be reviewed during the exercise.
A review can create a finding when the test identifies a gap or nonconformity.
A review can have workpapers attached as supporting evidence.
